name: RAG Knowledge-Base Q&A
description: Grounded, cited question answering over the platform knowledge base. Expands the user's question into multiple retrieval queries (multi_query), embeds it, semantically searches the shared_knowledge collection (knowledge_search), re-ranks the retrieved chunks for relevance (rerank), fact-checks the drafted answer against the retrieved evidence (grounding_check), synthesizes a cited HTML answer (llm_completion), runs it through guardrails, and publishes it as an openable artifact. The two quality nodes — multi_query and rerank — widen recall then tighten precision so the model sees the most relevant evidence first. Every claim is grounded against the retrieved chunks; nothing is invented.

meta.purpose: Grounded RAG Q&A that answers a question USING ONLY retrieved knowledge-base evidence, with multi-query recall expansion + cross-encoder-style rerank precision + grounding verification + citations.
how_it_works: 1. multi_query expands the user's question into N retrieval query variants (deterministic rule-based reformulation) so a single phrasing doesn't miss relevant chunks.
2. embedding vectorizes the question via /api/v1/embeddings (demonstrates the embed primitive; knowledge_search does its own server-side embedding for retrieval).
3. knowledge_search semantically searches the shared_knowledge Milvus collection for the question, returning ranked chunks.
4. rerank re-orders the retrieved chunks by lexical relevance to the question (deterministic BM25-style scorer) and keeps the top-N — turning recall@k into precision@k.
5. grounding_check verifies that the entities named in the question are present in the reranked evidence (deterministic, no second LLM call) so downstream synthesis can only cite real evidence.
6. llm_completion synthesizes a cited HTML answer from the reranked chunks, citing each claim with a [N] marker tied to the chunk source. It is instructed to answer ONLY from the provided evidence.
7. guardrails screens the synthesized answer for policy violations before it is published.
8. webhook_response renders the grounded, cited answer as an openable HTML artifact in the artifact rail.
meta.expected_output: Openable HTML artifact: the question header, an LLM-synthesized answer that cites the retrieved chunks with [N] markers, a retrieval-metadata footer (queries expanded, chunks retrieved, chunks kept after rerank, grounding verdict). The answer is grounded — every claim traces to a retrieved chunk.
meta.useful_when: Any internal-knowledge Q&A where a wrong/invented answer is worse than 'the knowledge base doesn't cover this'. The rerank + grounding_check gates make this a trust-grade RAG flow.

defaultInputs.question: What are the key features and architectural decisions behind OpenAgentic's Flows engine?

data.label: Synthesize cited answer
data.systemPrompt:
You are a retrieval-augmented question-answering assistant. You will receive a question and an array of retrieved knowledge-base chunks (each with content + score + source). Answer the question USING ONLY the provided chunks. Do NOT invent facts. If the chunks do not support an answer, say so directly. Output rules: - Emit semantic HTML only:

,

,

    /
  • , , ,
    . No // wrappers. - Open with a 1-2 sentence executive summary in a

    . - Break the answer into 1-3 sections with

    headers. - Cite EVERY factual claim with a [N] marker tied to the chunk's position in the provided array (chunk 1 is [1], etc.). Never invent a citation. - Close with a one-line

    Method: note: how many chunks were retrieved and whether the answer is well-supported. - Do NOT include any preamble, chain-of-thought, or 'here is the answer' wrapper — emit the HTML directly.

data.label: Publish cited answer
data.bodyTemplate:
Knowledge-Base Answer

Question: {{trigger.question}}

Queries expanded: {{steps.expand.count}} · Chunks retrieved: {{steps.search.result.resultCount}} · Chunks kept after rerank: {{steps.rerank.outputCount}} · Grounding: {{steps.ground.violationSummary}}

{{steps.synth.content}}

Grounded, cited answer from the OpenAgentic knowledge base. multi_query recall expansion + rerank precision + grounding_check verification.
